title: pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa
intro: Pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa, commonly known as a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, is a rare and lesser-known species. It has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The therapeutic actions of Pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa are believed to be due to its bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits and therapeutic actions of this plant.

The leaves of Pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa contain sesquiterpene lactones, including pilosine and pilosinol, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers of this plant contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been reported to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of Pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa also contain sesquiterpene lactones, including pilosine and pilosinol, as well as other bioactive compounds.

side_effects: Pilosella peleteriana subsp. tenuiscapa can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its saponin and sesquiterpene lactone content.
